sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: The reviewed pharmaceutical intelligence corpus for May 2026, encompassing major industry publications such as BioPharma Dive and STAT News, does not explicitly report on Proteolysis Targeting Chimeras (PROTAC) developments. While the provided articles detail significant advancements in gene editing technologies, antibody-drug conjugates, and obesity therapeutics, no specific clinical trials, partnerships, or regulatory submissions regarding PROTAC modalities were identified within the source text. Consequently, this briefing reflects the current absence of PROTAC-specific data in the selected literature, highlighting instead the broader oncology and biotechnology landscape dominated by gene editing and immunotherapy updates from entities including Merck, Eli Lilly, and Intellia Therapeutics. Stakeholders monitoring PROTAC pipelines should note that this month's public reporting focuses on alternative modalities, with no direct correlation to proteolysis targeting mechanisms found in the analyzed documents. This gap suggests that PROTAC-specific milestones may be reported through different channels or later in the development cycle, requiring continued surveillance beyond the current article set for comprehensive PROTAC intelligence. The absence of PROTAC data in these high-profile industry updates indicates a potential shift in reporting focus toward established modalities like CRISPR and ADCs during this period, alongside significant regulatory and public health shifts involving FDA leadership changes and global health emergencies.

points.text: Merck — ADC Program — Endometrial Cancer — No PROTAC data reported in ASCO26 abstracts.
Eli Lilly — Gene Editing — Hypercholesterolemia — No PROTAC pipeline reported in Verve updates.
Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR — Rare Swelling Disorder — No PROTAC mechanism identified in late-stage study.
Merck: Strategic objective to hit $70 billion in annual sales via oncology ADCs; no PROTAC program reported.
Eli Lilly: Strategic objective in obesity and gene editing; no PROTAC development identified in provided sources.
Intellia Therapeutics: Strategic objective for in vivo gene editing; no PROTAC technology utilized in current pipeline.
Merck ADC — Phase 3 — Endometrial Cancer — Trial hit mark; mechanism is ADC, not PROTAC.
Verve Therapeutics — Base Editing — Hypercholesterolemia — Lowered LDL; mechanism is gene editing, not PROTAC.
Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR — Rare Swelling Disorder — Late-stage success; mechanism is gene editing, not PROTAC.
